title: FIFA WC 2026 — 3rd Place Slot Assignment Logic
summary: 8 of the 12 groups' 3rd-place teams advance to the Round of 32. Each of the 8 R32 slots has a pre-set list of eligible groups (from official FIFA bracket). A group can appear in multiple slot lists, so we use constraint propagation to assign each group to exactly ONE slot.
why_repeats_happen: Group E appears in 6 slots, Group I in 6 slots. If we independently pick the best 3rd-place team per slot, the same team gets assigned to all 6 slots. This is wrong — each team can only play in ONE match.

constraint_propagation_algorithm.step_1: Find slots that have exactly 1 eligible group among the player's 8 picks. Assign immediately. Example: if K is picked → K must go to M80 (only option).
constraint_propagation_algorithm.step_2: Mark that group as 'used'. Remove it from all other slots' candidate lists.
constraint_propagation_algorithm.step_3: Repeat step 1-2 until no more forced assignments exist.
constraint_propagation_algorithm.step_4: For remaining ambiguous slots (multiple candidates), assign the group with the highest 3rd-place points.
constraint_propagation_algorithm.result: Every group is assigned to exactly ONE slot. No repeats.

forced_assignments.G: M77 or M85 (only 2 options — after M87 taken, if D not picked, M77 is free)

example.note: After K→M80 and L→M87, remove M80 and M87 from remaining candidates. Then propagate again to find next forced assignment.

groups_never_conflict_in_real_tournament: FIFA's slot design guarantees that for ANY valid set of 8 qualifying groups, each slot gets exactly one team. The constraint propagation finds that assignment. Tiebreaker (most points) is only needed if FIFA's real table is ambiguous for predicted picks.
